sanskrit: सर्वाणीन्द्रियकर्माणि प्राणकर्माणि चापरे । आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ जुह्वति ज्ञानदीपिते ॥
hindiTranslation: अन्य योगीलोग सम्पूर्ण इन्द्रियोंकी क्रियाओंको और प्राणोंकी क्रियाओंको ज्ञानसे प्रकाशित आत्मसंयमयोगरूप अग्निमें हवन किया करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: Others offer all the activities of the senses and the life-force into the fire of self-control, lit by knowledge.
essence: Knowledge turns self-control into a fire that consumes every impulse.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Some seekers train themselves so deeply that every sense activity and every life-force activity is offered into the fire of self-control, which is lit by knowledge.
simpleInsight: Real mastery begins when even your impulses no longer run the show.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen, and Krishna keeps unfolding the many forms of disciplined offering. After sense-restraint and mental sacrifice, he describes a subtler practice: turning all sensory and vital activity into self-mastery, with knowledge as the fire.
modernRelevance: Your hand reaches for the phone before you notice. Your breath shortens, your jaw tightens, your attention scatters. This verse points to a deeper training: not just stopping action, but mastering the energies that drive it.
translationLiteral: सर्वाणि (all) / इन्द्रियकर्माणि (activities of the senses) / प्राणकर्माणि (activities of the life-force) / च (and) / अपरे (others) / आत्मसंयमयोगाग्नौ (in the fire of the yoga of self-control) / जुह्वति (offer as an oblation) / ज्ञानदीपिते (kindled by knowledge)
insight: It shifts practice from suppressing behavior to governing the forces beneath behavior.
